reason: Core: defines the 'NotifierProvider<SealedState> when the sealed already owns loading/error' pattern that previewControllerProvider follows. The new canSaveProvider must derive a boolean from PreviewState via `is PreviewReady` (no AsyncValue wrapper). Also covers autoDispose controller + non-autoDispose cache contract that makes cache-hit re-entries immediately enable the save button.

Must add a new convention 'Save CTA gated by preview-ready state' in the five-section format (What / Why / Example / When to apply / Required tests). Existing 'Expensive-preview sliders submit on onChangeEnd, not onChanged' is the closest precedent — match its tone and shape.
Sealed-class dispatch rules — confirm `is PreviewReady` pattern (single-arm narrowing, not `switch` exhaustive) is acceptable for deriving a boolean from a sealed state.

Confirms new providers live next to siblings in the same feature/presentation/providers/ directory. canSaveProvider belongs in lib/features/export/presentation/providers/export_dispatch.dart adjacent to canExportProvider.
Test-shape conventions — equivalence-class coverage for PreviewState × isSaving × canExport in canSaveProvider tests, plus ProviderScope override patterns. Lint/format/analyze gate must remain clean.
Source of truth for requirements, decisions (ADR-lite), and out-of-scope boundaries. Re-read before each substantive edit.
